title: Hooks, Configuration, Credentials, and Repository Trust · Independent Repository Trial
summary: An independent maintainer receives a different graph, repository policy, and failure constraint and must transfer hooks, configuration, credentials, and repository trust into a new repository trust and local automation threat model. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of repository trust and local automation threat model with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Inspect configuration origin, scope, type, includes, conditional includes, worktree config, environment, and command overrides before trusting behavior.
Explain protected configuration and safe.directory ownership exceptions without using broad wildcards to conceal unsafe shared repositories.
Map client and server hook timing, working directory, environment, parameters, exit behavior, bypass paths, distribution, and enforcement limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
lab case — Case G92 is a lab in a disposable repository. conditional user configuration injects a credential helper and hooks path for this repository. The learner must predict scope and origin identify the exact protected or local source of each effective value, run git config --show-origin --show-scope --get-regexp "^(core|credential|include|safe)\.", explain a global includeIf sets credential.helper while local core.hooksPath points into repository data, test this changed condition: change repository ownership and refuse a wildcard safe.directory exception, and preserve this recovery boundary: capture effective config, remove narrow trust grants, and rotate any exposed credential.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inspect configuration origin, scope, type, includes, conditional includes, worktree config, environment, and command overrides before trusting behavior.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-untrusted-repository-defense-13016s
# scenario-key: trace-13016s probe-s61031 boundary-016s13 owner-13016sx fault-13016sz
# before-ref: main=a11ce13 candidate=b00b16s
# hypothesis: conditional user configuration injects a credential helper and hooks path for this repository
git config --show-origin --show-scope --get-regexp "^(core|credential|include|safe)\."
# expected: scope and origin identify the exact protected or local source of each effective value
# observation: a global includeIf sets credential.helper while local core.hooksPath points into repository data
# changed-case: change repository ownership and refuse a wildcard safe.directory exception
# after-ref: main=a11ce13 candidate=c0ffees
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: capture effective config, remove narrow trust grants, and rotate any exposed credential
# transfer-limit: inspect unknown repositories in isolation with hooks, filters, aliases, and external helpers disabled
